name: Smoke
description: Fast, high-confidence checks that confirm critical user journeys, core integrations, and baseline system availability are working after a change or deployment.
whenNotApplicable: changes that require only lower-level technical verification

name: Functional
description: Checks that business behavior, workflow rules, and user-visible feature outcomes work as intended.

name: Integration
description: Checks interactions between UI, services, APIs, and supporting dependencies.

name: API
description: Focused validation of request contracts, response structures, and error handling.
whenNotApplicable: pure presentation changes with no network impact

name: Regression
description: Checks that previously working behavior remains intact after change.

name: E2E
description: Checks full user journeys through multiple layers of the application stack.

name: Performance
description: Checks responsiveness, rendering speed, and throughput under expected load.

name: Security
description: Checks confidentiality, integrity, authorization, and safe failure behavior.

name: Accessibility
description: Checks keyboard support, semantics, contrast, and assistive-technology usability.

name: Usability
description: Checks whether the product is clear, efficient, discoverable, and forgiving for intended users, including feedback quality and error recovery.
whenApplicable: multi-step tasks where guidance and feedback matter
whenNotApplicable: technical refactors with no user-facing impact
changes limited to invisible infrastructure behavior

name: Responsiveness
description: Checks that layouts, navigation, readability, and interactions remain usable and visually stable across mobile, tablet, laptop, and desktop screen sizes.
whenApplicable: dashboards, tables, and forms with dense content
navigation patterns that change across breakpoints
orientation changes or browser window resizing
components vulnerable to overflow, clipping, or reflow issues
whenNotApplicable: changes proven to affect only server-side processing with no viewport impact

name: Compatibility
description: Checks that behavior remains correct and consistent across supported browsers and client environments, especially Chrome, Firefox, Microsoft Edge, and Safari.
whenApplicable: browser-sensitive controls such as date pickers, uploads, tables, modals, and navigation
features that depend on layout, rendering, focus handling, downloads, or clipboard behavior
changes that may behave differently in Chrome, Firefox, Microsoft Edge, or Safari
whenNotApplicable: headless background processing with no browser-facing surface
changes proven independent of browser rendering and client APIs

name: Data Integrity
description: Checks that saved, transformed, and displayed data remains correct and consistent.

name: Recovery
description: Checks graceful behavior after transient failures, retries, or interrupted actions.
